perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential Information in confidence and to use it solely for the purposes contemplated herein and agrees further that the Confidential Information shall not be used for any other purpose nor disclosed to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party. Each party agrees it shall apply the same level of confidential treatment to the other party's Confidential Information as it does to its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to use reasonable efforts to hold the other's Confidential Information in confidence and to use it solely for the purposes contemplated herein and agrees further that the Confidential Information shall not be used for any other purpose nor disclosed to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party. Each party agrees it shall apply the same level of confidential treatment to the other party's Confidential Information as it does to its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "agrees to hold...in confidence" to "agrees to use reasonable efforts to hold...in confidence" weakens the confidentiality obligation. This introduces ambiguity and creates a contradiction with section 8, which states, "disclosure of any Confidential Information in violation of the terms hereof would cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party." The "reasonable efforts" clause suggests that some level of disclosure might be acceptable, contradicting the 'irreparable harm' clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereby acknowledge that disclosure of any Confidential Information in violation of the terms hereof would cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party. Without prejudice to the rights and remedies otherwise available, each party is entitled to equitable relief through injunction if the other party or any of its employees, agents or representatives breaches any provision of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each party 'agrees to hold... in confidence', implying a strict obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation by stating that each party 'agrees to use reasonable efforts to hold...in confidence'. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that 'disclosure of any Confidential Information in violation of the terms hereof would cause irreparable harm'. The 'reasonable efforts' clause implies that some level of disclosure might be acceptable, contradicting the 'irreparable harm' clause, as a violation could occur even with 'reasonable efforts'.

perturbation.original_text: The parties may disclose the Confidential Information to their respective officers, directors, employees, attorneys, subsidiaries and affiliates working in connection with the purpose for which the Confidential Information is disclosed but only to the extent necessary to carry out that purpose and subject to all requirements of confidentiality set forth in this Agreement. The party receiving the Confidential Information (the "Receiving Party") may also disclose --------------- Confidential Information to a third party pursuant to paragraph 4(g) below and subject to the notice provision set forth therein.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties may disclose the Confidential Information to their respective officers, directors, employees, attorneys, subsidiaries and affiliates working in connection with the purpose for which the Confidential Information is disclosed but only to the extent necessary to carry out that purpose. The party receiving the Confidential Information (the "Receiving Party") may also disclose --------------- Confidential Information to a third party pursuant to paragraph 4(g) below and subject to the notice provision set forth therein.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "and subject to all requirements of confidentiality set forth in this Agreement", the confidentiality requirements are waived for internal disclosure. This now creates a contradiction with section 2, which states that "Each party agrees it shall apply the same level of confidential treatment to the other party's Confidential Information as it does to its own Confidential Information." Now, disclosing to internal parties would not have the same confidentiality level, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential Information in confidence and to use it solely for the purposes contemplated herein and agrees further that the Confidential Information shall not be used for any other purpose nor disclosed to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party. Each party agrees it shall apply the same level of confidential treatment to the other party's Confidential Information as it does to its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes 'and subject to all requirements of confidentiality set forth in this Agreement', which ensures internal disclosures are subject to the agreement's confidentiality terms. The changed text removes this phrase, waiving confidentiality requirements for internal disclosures. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' section, which states that 'Each party agrees it shall apply the same level of confidential treatment to the other party's Confidential Information as it does to its own Confidential Information.' The change allows for potentially different levels of confidentiality for internal versus external disclosures, creating a clear and direct conflict and therefore a strong in-text contradiction, according to the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of one (1) year from the effective date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect unless terminated earlier by either party with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term length to "unless terminated earlier by either party with 30 days written notice" now creates a contradiction with section 14. Where the original text stated, "No amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be valid or binding on the parties unless made in writing and signed on behalf of each of the parties by its authorized officer or representative.", but here it can be terminated unilaterally with just written notice.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties in connection with the subject matter hereof and supersedes and replaces all prior discussions, understandings and agreements (oral or written) related thereto. No amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be valid or binding on the parties unless made in writing and signed on behalf of each of the parties by its authorized officer or representative.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a fixed term of one year, while the changed text allows for termination by either party with 30 days' notice. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that no amendment or modification is valid unless in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text introduces a unilateral termination option, effectively modifying the agreement without requiring both parties' signatures, creating a clear and direct conflict as explained.

perturbation.original_text: Any notice or other communication made or given by either party in connection with this Agreement shall be sent via registered or certified mail, postage prepaid, return receipt requested; by facsimile transmission, with confirmation of receipt; or by overnight courier service to the other party as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: Any notice or other communication made or given by either party in connection with this Agreement shall be sent via email. An email confirmation is required, and if there is no confirmation within 48 hours, parties should then use registered or certified mail, postage prepaid, return receipt requested; by facsimile transmission, with confirmation of receipt; or by overnight courier service to the other party as follows:
perturbation.explanation: By adding email as primary contact, this now creates an in-text contradiction with section 12. In section 12 is only allows for written registered/certified mail, facsimile with confirmation, or overnight courier. Adding email as a viable primary source of communication directly violates the method to communicating to parties under section 12.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any notice or other communication made or given by either party in connection with this Agreement shall be sent via registered or certified mail, postage prepaid, return receipt requested; by facsimile transmission, with confirmation of receipt; or by overnight courier service to the other party as follows: If to Kaplan: If to Company: Ross Hamachek Vince Pisano ----------------------------- Kaplan, Inc. Quest Education Corp. ----------------------------- 888 Seventh Avenue, 23rd Fl. 1400 Hembree Road #100 ----------------------------- New York, NY 10106 Roswell, GA 30076 ----------------------------- Facsimile: 212-492-5860 Copy to: Copy to: Veronica Dillon, Esq. Morris Brown, Esq. ----------------------------- General Counsel Greenberg Traurig ----------------------------- Kaplan , Inc. 777 S. Flagler Dr. #300-E ----------------------------- 888 Seventh Avenue, 23rd Fl. West Palm Beach, FL 33401 ----------------------------- New York, NY 10106 ----------------------------- Facsimile: 212-492-5860 Any party may by notice as set forth in this paragraph change the address to which further notices to it shall be mailed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces email as a primary method of communication. The contradicted text, which is the original text of the changed text, explicitly lists registered/certified mail, facsimile, or overnight courier as the means of communication. The addition of email directly contradicts the established methods in the contradicted text, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as it creates uncertainty which method of communication is the official form, which adheres to the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The parties hereby consent and submit to personal jurisdiction of courts situated in New York County, New York, and all disputes arising in connection with the Agreement shall be heard only by a court of competent subject matter jurisdiction situated in New York County, New York.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties hereby consent and submit to personal jurisdiction of courts situated in New York County, New York, unless the dispute involves intellectual property rights, in which case disputes will be resolved through arbitration in Delaware. All disputes arising in connection with the Agreement shall be heard only by a court of competent subject matter jurisdiction situated in New York County, New York.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "unless the dispute involves intellectual property rights, in which case disputes will be resolved through arbitration in Delaware", it creates a contradiction with section 13. It states, "all disputes arising in connection with the Agreement shall be heard only by a court of competent subject matter jurisdiction situated in New York County, New York." Now disputes involving IP will be arbitrated in Deleware, directly violating the initial clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York without regard to conflict of laws principles. The parties hereby consent and submit to personal jurisdiction of courts situated in New York County, New York, unless the dispute involves intellectual property rights, in which case disputes will be resolved through arbitration in Delaware. All disputes arising in connection with the Agreement shall be heard only by a court of competent subject matter jurisdiction situated in New York County, New York.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception for intellectual property disputes, which will now be resolved through arbitration in Delaware. The contradicted text states that "all disputes arising in connection with the Agreement shall be heard only by a court of competent subject matter jurisdiction situated in New York County, New York." This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text now allows for some disputes to be resolved outside of New York courts, violating the original clause.
